label: 1621b Health promotion and disease prevention services

L. 100–713, title II, § 203(a)102 Stat. 4804Nov. 23, 1988“The Congress finds that health promotion and disease prevention activities will—“(1)improve the health and well being of Indians, and“(2)reduce the expenses for medical care of Indians.”

children.heading: Authorization
children.label: a Authorization
children.text: The Secretary, acting through the Service, shall provide health promotion and disease prevention services to Indians so as to achieve the health status objectives set forth in section 1602(b) of this title.1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Evaluation statement for Presidential budget
children.label: b Evaluation statement for Presidential budget

children.text: the health promotion and disease prevention needs of Indians,
the health promotion and disease prevention activities which would best meet such needs,
the internal capacity of the Service to meet such needs, and
the resources which would be required to enable the Service to undertake the health promotion and disease prevention activities necessary to meet such needs.
